attack_surface.exposure: Goal-channel body can write back to its own (or a cycling) channel name; without isolation this re-enters InvokeGoal to stack-overflow.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Runtime call graph; no external ingress on this branch.
mitigations: AppChannels.Get returns null when an executing goal-channel resolves, surfacing ChannelNotFound at the writer
try/finally restores prev across awaited goal body and on catchable exceptions
Per-channel flag, not per-actor — concurrent calls to different goal channels isolate
gaps: AppChannels.Channel(string) opportunistic-write API does not consult IsExecuting; bypass-able if any caller writes to its own goal-channel name.

attack_surface.exposure: Stale callers of FreezeFoundational/Snapshot/PushChannelsOverride would silently break or bypass the new guard.
mitigations: Source-tree grep clean across PLang, PLang.Tests, PlangConsole (only stale obj/ metadata remains).

attack_surface.exposure: PrimitiveConversionFailed now mutates the inbound errors.Error.ErrorChain when source is an Error.
mitigations: Mutation is on the inbound Error instance, no shared accumulator concurrency
Message format unchanged; no new sensitive-data egress paths

gaps: Tagged._cache remains unbounded (pre-existing, tracked in branch memory).

findings.vector: AppChannels.Channel(name) — the NoOp-fallback opportunistic-write variant — resolves channels via raw _channels.TryGetValue, skipping the IsExecuting check. A caller writing via this API to the same goal-channel that hosts the current execution recurses past the guard until the CallStack backstop fires.
findings.preconditions: Caller writes via Channels.Channel(name) (not Get/Resolve) AND that name is registered as a goal-channel AND the current async context is already executing that goal channel's body.
findings.impact: Local DoS — unbounded call-frame churn during a build, contained by RecursionDepthExceeded backstop. No code execution, no data egress.
findings.exploit_sketch: Today: not reachable. Only caller is file/read.cs:76 writing 'builder' warnings, and BuilderChannel.goal body is '- write out %!data%' (does not read files). Adding any future Channels.Channel(name) caller whose name overlaps with a goal-channel that may execute file IO closes the loop.
findings.proposed_fix: In AppChannels.Channel(name): if (channel is channel.goal.@this g && g.IsExecuting) return NoOp; — mirror the Get-side guard, fall through to the existing no-op sink rather than the executing channel.

summary: Branch is a security improvement. Channel recursion guard re-implemented with per-channel AsyncLocal<bool> IsExecuting observed at AppChannels.Get; the AppChannels.Snapshot / actor-override layer is deleted with no source-tree stragglers. Mutation evidence (tester v2) corroborates the guard arms the regression test. One Low — Channels.Channel(name) is parity-gapped vs Get and could be armed by a future caller; close before any new opportunistic-write site lands.
